messages.content:
You inventory a golden report's data-driven content and bind it to warehouse data.
Inventory EVERY narration-driving value (figures, counts, amounts, percentages, data-derived dates — not styling numbers) and EVERY table.
For each item propose {familyId, sql} that reproduces it, or binding: null with a reason.
Use :period in SQL for periodic tables so bindings transfer across periods. Probe with run_sql BEFORE submitting.
Item shape: {id (snake_case, stable), kind: "value"|"table", anchor: {sectionKey, blockIndex, spanIndex|null}, raw, parsed, binding, reason}.
Anchors come from the bracketed coordinates in the document below: [b0.s1] → blockIndex 0, spanIndex 1; [b1] table → blockIndex 1, spanIndex null.

Data catalog:
revenue — "Revenue" (periodic, quarter; filed: 2026-Q1, 2026-Q2)
  fam_rev(channel TEXT, amount REAL) — 25 rows (2026-Q1: 10, 2026-Q2: 15)

Golden period: 2026-Q2

Document:
title: Q2 Revenue Report
## section: overview — Overview
[b0.s0] "Revenue climbed sharply this quarter, driven primarily by paid search and a resurgent social channel that together accounted for the overwhelming majority of ne"
[b0.s1] "The CFO called it \"solid\" — up 12% ✓ over last year."
[b1] table (2 cols × 15 rows): channel | amount
  channel_1 | 1000
  channel_2 | 2000
  channel_3 | 3000
  channel_4 | 4000
  channel_5 | 5000
  channel_6 | 6000
  channel_7 | 7000
  channel_8 | 8000
  channel_9 | 9000
  channel_10 | 10000
  channel_11 | 11000
  channel_12 | 12000
  … 3 more rows

These are verified binding patterns from other periods of the same report family. Try these first: the same logical value binds with the same SQL at a different :period. Reuse their item ids for matching items.
period 2026-Q1:
  rev_total: "$3.8M" ← fam_rev: SELECT sum(amount) FROM fam_rev WHERE _period = :period
Build and submit the binding inventory.

function.description: Run one read-only SQL SELECT against the project's warehouse. :period binds to THIS GOLDEN's period. Results capped at 200 rows.
function.name: run_sql

function.description: Submit the final binding inventory. Calling this with a valid inventory ends the task.
function.name: submit_inventory
